raw_title: Self-Censorship in Journalism: The Role of Emotional, Professional, and Institutional Factors
title_en: Self-Censorship in Journalism: The Role of Emotional, Professional, and Institutional Factors
title_zh: Self-Censorship in Journalism: The Role of Emotional, Professional, and Institutional Factors
plain_summary_en: This paper studies Self-Censorship in Journalism: The Role of Emotional, Professional, and Institutional Factors. In plain terms, it asks what is happening, why it matters for public communication, and what evidence the authors use. The likely focus is Work (physics), Government (linguistics), Variety (cybernetics), Qualitative research. The abstract suggests a mixed methods approach, which makes the work useful for understanding how communication changes in everyday life.

research_direction_en: The paper points to an active research direction around Work (physics), Government (linguistics), Variety (cybernetics), Qualitative research, especially how institutions, platforms, and audiences shape communication outcomes.
